section_map.title: Introduction / Early topological-insulator definition
section_map.summary: A topological insulator has a bulk energy gap separating occupied and empty bands, but its edge or surface necessarily carries gapless states protected by time-reversal symmetry; source anchor: Introduction (lines 83-90).

section_map.id: Topological Band Theory / Bloch-state setup for insulating crystals
section_map.title: Topological Band Theory / Bloch-state setup for insulating crystals
section_map.summary: Band theory describes crystal states by crystal momentum k in the Brillouin zone, with Bloch states |u_m(k)> defined in a unit cell as eigenstates of the Bloch Hamiltonian H(k), and eigenvalues E_m(k) defining the energy bands; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The insulating state (lines 161-168).

section_map.id: Topological Band Theory / Gap-preserving interpolation defines equivalence
section_map.title: Topological Band Theory / Gap-preserving interpolation defines equivalence
section_map.summary: Two insulating states are topologically equivalent when the Hamiltonian can be tuned continuously between them without closing the energy gap; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The insulating state (lines 174-177).

section_map.id: Topological Band Theory / Stable equivalence of conventional insulators to the vacuum
section_map.title: Topological Band Theory / Stable equivalence of conventional insulators to the vacuum
section_map.summary: Under the paper's stable classification that identifies states differing only by trivial core bands, all conventional insulators are equivalent to one another and to the vacuum; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The insulating state (lines 178-187).

section_map.id: Quantum Hall state / Integer Hall conductivity equation
section_map.title: Quantum Hall state / Integer Hall conductivity equation
section_map.summary: If N Landau levels are filled and the rest are empty, the Hall conductivity is sigma_xy = N e^2 / h; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The quantum Hall state (lines 217-224, Eq. qhall).
